calendar.matters: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Minute Order Reffering Cast To Pro Bono Mediation
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Continued To Aug-12-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nathan Borris and Ann Kavanaugh for petitioner Stephanie Enright, (via Zoom).
Mark Kelly for respondent Antonio Enright, who was also present (via Zoom).
Ericka Enright in pro per, and as "attorney-in-fact" under power of attorney of objector Martin Enright (via Zoom).
Anne Steele for petitioner Lusyana Enright (via Zoom).

Judge Russell S. Roeca disclosed that he represented the law firm JKZ in the past.

The Court noted that Ericka Enright was not an attorney and cautioned her against engaging in the unauthorized practice of law.

Discussion was held regarding no communication from the trustee, that the trustee has been residing at the property for almost 5 years not paying any rent, that there has been no formal accounting filed, and that trustee Antonio Enright's objections require his verified objection to be filed and served.

The Court ordered the accounting to be filed by the next calling of this matter.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 22, 2026. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by July 29, 2029. The matter is continued to August 12, 2026 and the Parties are to return on August 12, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Continued To Apr-15-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Settlement
Off Calendar Mar-18-2026 Off Calendar

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-12-2025 Continued To Mar-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Reimbursement Of Expenses
Off Calendar Nov-13-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Nathan Borris for petitioner Stephanie Enright, who was also present (via Zoom).
Mark Kelly for respondent Antonio Enright, who was also present (via Zoom).
Ericka Enright in pro per, and as "attorney-in-fact" under power of attorney of objector Martin Enright (via Zoom).
Anne Steele for petitioner Lusyana Enright (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Lusyana Enright's Petition for Reimbursement of Expenses; and 2) Stephanie Enright's Petition for Accounting, Demand for Distribution, for Breach of Trust, and Appointment of Independent Trustee.

The Court noted that Ericka Enright was not an attorney and cautioned her against engaging in the unauthorized practice of law.

The Court first addressed Lusyana Enright's petition. The Court stated its intention to grant reimbursement for HOA dues and property taxes, but deny additional requests for plumbing, garbage disposal, etc. Mr. Borris objected to the petition under Probate Code section 1043, arguing that the petition lacked proof and that rent owed by Ms. Enright should offset any reimbursement. Ericka Enright responded that rent obligations remained enforceable and that waiver or estoppel did not apply.
After hearing arguments from the parties, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant reimbursement in the amount of $19,364.74 for HOA dues and property taxes, which should have been paid by the trustee. The Court denied reimbursement for garbage, utilities, and junk-removal expenses, as well as any request for attorney's fees or prejudgment interest, finding no entitlement or sufficient showing.

The Court then addressed the petition filed by Stephanie Enright. Counsel reported that, with resolution of the reimbursement issue, the parties expected to finalize accounting figures and move toward full settlement. They agreed that mediation was unnecessary.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to March 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 for status regarding settlement.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-07-2025 Continued To Nov-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Continued To Oct-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Nathan Borris, Esq., for Stephanie Enright, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., for Antonio Enright, Respondent.
Ericka Enright, for Martin Enright, interested Party.

At the court's query, counsels provide updates regarding the status of settlement discussions. Mr. Kelly reports that the parties are close to resolution and that he does not anticipate filing a petition for court approval of a settlement agreement. After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Oct-07-2025. Mr. Borris advises that should the parties reach an agreement prior to the next calling, he intends to file a declaration to advise the court.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Peou. Not Reported.

Petition For Demand Of Property Tax Reimbursement
Off Calendar Apr-30-2025 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Continued To Jul-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark Kelly for and with Antonio Enright, Respondent (via Zoom).
Nathan Borris for and with Stephanie Enright, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Erica Enright, appearing on behalf of Martin Enright, Petitioner.

Erica Enright appeared on behalf of Martin Enright as Power of Attorney and stated funds had not yet been reimbursed and requested the Court's assistance. Mr. Borris stated that his client had no objection to the requested relief and waived any procedural objections. Mr. Kelly also had no objection but noted that Lusyana Enright, represented by an attorney named Ann Steele, may have concerns regarding reimbursements but has not appeared. Mr. Kelly requested Court approval to avoid future objections to the payment.

The Court indicated that it would treat Martin Enright's Petition for Demand of Property Tax Reimbursement as a Probate Code section 17200(b)(6) petition and took the matter under submission.

The Court then heard updates regarding the Petition for Accounting, etc. filed on behalf of Stephanie Enright. Mr. Borris explained that he and Mr. Kelly had been working collaboratively and proposed that the trustee reserve funds equivalent to any disputed reimbursement claim, so that the remaining trust administration could proceed. Mr. Kelly agreed and noted that the estate was awaiting final escape tax assessments from San Mateo County.

The Court confirmed with counsel that no additional mediation was needed. Counsel requested a continuance to allow time to resolve creditor claims and settle outstanding tax obligations. The Court continued Stephanie Enright's Petition to July 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-30-2024 Continued To Oct-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Borris appeared by zoom for Stephanie Enright (petitioner).
Mark Kelly appeared by zoom for Antonio Enright (trustee) by zoom.

Mr. Kelly stated that they are making very good progress with administration of this trust, that they closed escrow on the sale of the Colma property, that they are working on the claims that have been made, that they are working on reimbursements, that they will then make a plan for distribution and that everybody knows what's going on. The Petition for Accounting, Demand for Distribution, etc. is continued to October 29, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is to be filed by October 22, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-16-2024 Continued To Jul-30-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Nathan Borris, Esq., for Stephanie Enright, Petitioner.
Mark Kelly, Esq., for Antonio Enright, Respondent.
Anne Steele, Esq., for Lusyana Enright, Beneficiary.

At the outset, Mr. Kelly reports of the pending sale of real property and requests a continuance to allow the preceding to be finalized. Mr. Borris agrees with Mr. Kelly's report and request for a continuance.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Jul-30-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The parties are instructed to file a joint status report one week prior to the next calling.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Jaime. Not Reported.

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-09-2024 Continued To Apr-16-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Moon Kim appeared via zoom for Stephanie Enright (petitioner).
Mark Kelly in person for and with Antonio Enright (respondent).
Anne Stelle appeared via zoom for Lusyana Enright (beneficiary).

Discussion was held regarding the sale of the property located in Colma, that all parties agree to sell the house, and that Mr. Kelly will provide information on a small account of $2500.00 cash assets. The Court commends the parties of doing a great job of working with each other. The matter is continued to April 16, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Visiting Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-26-2023 Continued To Jan-09-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Moon Kim appeared via zoom for Stephanie Enright (petitioner).
Mark Kelly in person for and with Antonio Enright (respondent).
Anne Stelle appeared via zoom for Lusyana Enright (beneficiary).

Discussion was held regarding the two properties being the only asset in the trust, that both properties are similar in value, that this was the first time Mr. Kelly has heard of parties interested in selling the properties, that he would like his clients to execute his role and sell the houses, and that they need to submit an accounting and prepare a plan for distribution. The Matter is continued to January 09, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed by January 02, 2024.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.
